findings.notes: VACUUM INTO is SQLite-specific; Turso/libSQL does not support it. Checkpoint creation must use alternative serialization (e.g., file copy or backup API).
VACUUM INTO on attached database (active_vec). Turso lacks ATTACH and VACUUM INTO; vector shard snapshot needs complete reimplementation.
Used to resolve main/active_vec paths. Turso has no ATTACH or PRAGMA database_list; shard path resolution must be externalized.

findings.signature: SELECT 1 AS found FROM temp.sqlite_master WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
findings.notes: Queries temp.sqlite_master for temp table detection. Turso may not expose temp schema the same way.

findings.signature: SELECT 1 AS found FROM active_vec.sqlite_master WHERE type IN ('table','view') AND name = 'vec_memories'
findings.notes: Queries attached database sqlite_master. Turso lacks ATTACH; attached-database introspection must be replaced.
FTS5 'delete-all' command. Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5; this must be reimplemented with external search or alternative FTS.
FTS5 'rebuild' command used in post-restore derived artifact rebuilds. FTS5 not available in Turso.

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, ...); END
findings.notes: CREATE TRIGGER for append-only enforcement. Turso supports triggers but syntax may differ; verify compatibility.

findings.signature: database.exec(`SAVEPOINT ${savepointName}`); ... ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT; RELEASE SAVEPOINT
findings.notes: SAVEPOINT used for atomic merge-restore of individual tables. Turso supports savepoints but verify semantics.
WAL checkpoint pragma. Turso/libSQL may use different WAL or no WAL; post-delete maintenance needs adaptation.

findings.symbol: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5
findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, content='memory_index', content_rowid='id')
findings.notes: FTS5 virtual table with content-sync. Turso has no FTS5 and no loadExtension; schema downgrade path must remove FTS entirely.

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_insert/update/delete AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ON memory_index
findings.notes: FTS5 content-sync triggers. All three triggers reference FTS5 commands; entire FTS sync layer must be replaced for Turso.
PRAGMA .pragma() method is better-sqlite3 specific. Turso uses SET statements; foreign_keys toggle needs API change.

findings.signature: readonly array of 39 column names defining v15 memory_index schema
findings.notes: Schema version constants define column sets. Migration to Turso must preserve version-aware column lists.

findings.signature: db.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN bm25_repair_needed INTEGER DEFAULT 0')
findings.notes: Dynamic ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for schema evolution. Turso supports ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN but verify column-default semantics.

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS consolidation_state (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY CHECK(id = 1), last_run_at TEXT)
findings.notes: Single-row cadence table with CHECK constraint. Turso supports CHECK but verify CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS idempotency.
Manual BEGIN IMMEDIATE for write-lock acquisition. Turso's distributed locking model differs; explicit transaction begin may behave differently.
SQLite datetime() with string concatenation for interval arithmetic. Turso supports datetime() but verify || concatenation in date modifiers.
INDEXED BY hint for partial index usage. Turso may not support INDEXED BY hints or partial indexes identically.
PRAGMA incremental_vacuum after auto_vacuum=2. Turso/libSQL has different space reclamation; .pragma() API unavailable.
